key_insight: Prototype-guided latent alignment improves fine-tuning of molecular foundation models by anchoring target atoms to chemically similar source prototypes, boosting generalisation in low-data settings.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to adapt pretrained ML interatomic potentials to new materials systems with fewer DFT-labelled examples and lower overfitting risk.
method: Cluster atoms by similar latent chemical environments to define source-domain prototypes.
Align each target atom’s energy contribution to the nearest prototype to preserve useful pretrained interactions.
Add an inductive bias during fine-tuning without assuming the target chemistry matches the source exactly.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on rMD17 with MACE and SchNet across varying data budgets, and on MACE-OFF foundation models for SPICE, with up to 18% lower energy MAE than standard fine-tuning in the low-data regime.
watch_item: Prototype quality and latent-space clustering may be sensitive to representation choice and could require careful tuning to avoid misalignment on very novel chemistries.
paper_title: Prototype-Guided Latent Alignment for Data-Efficient Fine-Tuning of Molecular Foundation Models
